markdown.markdown:
::: {.landing-hero} Evaluation notes This section collects source-grounded study notes from the MCP research library's `evaluation-texts` collection. The notes combine the existing hand-authored Purposeful Program Theory section, chapter-level Rossi/Lipsey, Chen, Impact Evaluation in Practice, Realistic Evaluation, and Qualitative Research & Evaluation Methods and Handbook of Practical Program Evaluation notes, step-level Patton/U-FE notes, and generated notes for the other ready evaluation texts.
10 books Evaluation texts MCP sourced Export BibTeX
::: ```{=html}

``` ::: {.section-grid} ::: {.section-card data-note-card="" data-book="all" data-type="Synthesis"}
SynthesisAll texts
### [Comparing Evaluation Texts](evaluation-texts-compared.qmd) A cross-text synthesis of the main evaluation sources and when each should lead. ::: ::: {.section-card data-note-card="" data-book="all" data-type="Synthesis"}
SynthesisMagenta Book
### [Which Evaluation Model Is the Magenta Book Closest To?](magenta-book-evaluation-model-alignment.qmd) A balanced comparison of the Magenta Book against CIPP, Rossi/Lipsey/Freeman, utilization-focused evaluation, and related models. ::: ::: {.section-card data-note-card="" data-book="all" data-type="Synthesis"}
SynthesisAll texts
### [Is a Theory of Change Needed in an Evaluation?](theory-of-change-needed-in-evaluation.qmd) A critical assessment of when evaluations need a theory of change, when a lighter model is enough, and when theory work becomes performative. ::: ::: {.section-card data-note-card="" data-book="purposeful-programme-theory" data-type="Book"}
Book2011
